reasoning: To reach $140,000 by December 31, 2025, Bitcoin needs a ~27% increase from its early November price of ~$110,000 within 52 days. While historically possible, especially in Q4 of a post-halving year, recent momentum has turned negative—Bitcoin's "Uptober" trend broke in 2025 as the price fell from an early October ATH of $125,000 to ~$110,000 by November. Major institutions like Galaxy Digital recently downgraded their year-end price targets from $185,000 to $120,000, citing continued ETF selling pressure and macroeconomic headwinds. A quantitative barrier-touch model (assuming 60% volatility and zero to moderate drift) gives a theoretical probability of roughly 25-35%. Taking into account the negative near-term trend against the historical tendency of sudden crypto breakouts, a 30% probability optimally balances these factors.
